questions.question: Which of the following class of organisms belongs to saprotrophs?
questions.studentAnswer: Fungi
questions.correctAnswer: a) Fungi

questions.question: Which part of plant is called food factory?
questions.studentAnswer: Leaves
questions.correctAnswer: c) Leaves

questions.question: The way of taking food by bees is
questions.studentAnswer: Sucking
questions.correctAnswer: b) Sucking

questions.question: The false feet of Amoeba are used for
questions.studentAnswer: movement only
questions.correctAnswer: c) Capturing food and movement
questions.feedback: Pseudopodia (false feet) are used for both movement and capturing prey.

proof.text: occurs in a long tube called the alimentary canal. This process starts in the mouth and ends at the anus (Fig. 9.1). As food moves through this canal, digestive juices secreted at different parts break it down into simpler forms. This simpler form of food is absorbed by different parts of our alimentary canal and transported to various parts of our body to carry out various functions. 9.1 Nutrition in Animals How do the complex food components get broken down into simpler forms and used by the body in various animals? Is this process the same in all animals or does it vary? Let us fi rst try to understand this process in humans. 9.1.1 Digestion in human beings Let us trace the journey of food inside our body as it passes through diff erent parts of the alimentary canal. Beginning with the mouth cavity The journey of the food you eat begins when it enters your mouth. Your teeth break down

questions.question: The shape of the guard cells in stomata …………
questions.studentAnswer: semi-circular
questions.correctAnswer: a) Bean shaped
questions.feedback: Guard cells are typically bean-shaped or kidney-shaped.

proof.text: Life Processes in Plants 147 Activity 10.6: Let us examine (demonstration activity) The teacher may demonstrate this activity.  Collect a leaf from a plant such as rhoeo, money plant, onion, hibiscus, coleus, or any grass.  Put it in a beaker fi lled with water.  Carefully peel a thin layer from the lower surface of the leaf.  Place the peel in a watch glass with water.  Now, take a microscope slide and carefully put a drop of water on it.  Using forceps, transfer the peel of the leaf from the watch glass to the slide with the help of forceps.  Put a drop of ink on the leaf peel with the help of a dropper.  Cover the peel with a coverslip and observe it under a microscope. What do you observe? Do you notice tiny pores on the peel, as shown in Fig. 10.6? These pores are called stomata. Stomata, present on the surface of leaves, help in the exchange of gases. 10.3 Transport in Plan

questions.question: Which material is a poor conductor of heat?
questions.studentAnswer: Wool
questions.correctAnswer: c) Wool

questions.question: What is the primary reason woollen clothes keep us warm?
questions.studentAnswer: They trap air
questions.correctAnswer: c) They trap air.

questions.question: Which part of the clinical thermometer contains mercury?
questions.studentAnswer: The tube
questions.correctAnswer: c) The bulb
questions.feedback: Mercury is stored in the bulb at the end of the thermometer.

proof.text: 7.6. Make sure that the bulbs of the thermometer are immersed in soil and water, and do not touch the bottoms or the sides of the bowls.  Place the set-up in sunlight. Fig. 7.6: Measurement of the temperature of soil and water Soil Stand Water Thermometer  Observe the movement of the coloured streak in the water.  As you supply heat, a streak of colour starts moving up and then coming down from the sides (Fig. 7.5b). Why does the streak of coloured water go up in the middle and come down from the sides? The water at the bottom of the beaker gets heated up and becomes hot. It expands, becomes lighter, and rises. The water on the sides of the beaker is comparatively cooler and heavier, and comes down to take the place of the rising water. Then, this water gets heated and in turn also rises. This cycle continues until the entire volume of water gets heated. In this case, the entire volum

questions.question: Assertion (A) : Tongue is fleshy muscular organ. Reason (R) : Tongue is used for talking.
questions.studentAnswer: A is true & R is false
questions.correctAnswer: b) Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
questions.feedback: Both statements are true facts about the tongue, but talking is not the reason why it is a muscular organ.

questions.question: Assertion (A) : Green colour in leaves help in photosynthesis. Reason (R) : Photosynthesis results in formation of starch.
questions.studentAnswer: Both A & R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
questions.correctAnswer: b) Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A

questions.question: Define Temperature? Mention the device that is used to measure temperature?
questions.studentAnswer: A temperature is an hot or cold of an body / object. The device that is used to measure temperature is thermometer
questions.correctAnswer: Temperature is the degree of hotness or coldness of an object. A thermometer is used to measure it.

questions.question: What is the function of Tongue? (OR)
questions.studentAnswer: The tongue helps for chewing & swallowing.
questions.correctAnswer: The tongue helps in mixing saliva with food, chewing, swallowing, and speaking.

questions.question: Define Nutrition? Mention the modes of nutrition?
questions.studentAnswer: The process where food is taken & used for growth, energy, body building & repair for our body. There are two types of nutrition: Autotrophic nutrients, heterotrophic nutrients.
questions.correctAnswer: Nutrition is the mode of taking food by an organism and its utilization by the body. Modes: Autotrophic and Heterotrophic.

questions.question: Why do organisms need to take food?
questions.studentAnswer: Organisms need to take food due to they are depend on each other / other trophics like (plants & animals) like we humans, animals & un-green plants.
questions.correctAnswer: Organisms need food for energy, growth, repair of damaged parts, and to carry out life processes.
questions.feedback: Correct, but mention energy and growth more explicitly for full marks.

proof.text: Nutrition in Plants - Grade 7 NCERT Science. All organisms require food. Plants can make their food themselves but animals including humans cannot. They get it from plants or animals that eat plants. Thus, humans and animals are directly or indirectly dependent on plants.

questions.question: What kind of clothes should we wear in summer & winter.
questions.studentAnswer: Summer: we should wear very loose clothes & cooler clothes due to in summer heat presence is more (Cotton). Winter: we should wear very thick & woolen clothes or cotton clothes which traps the heat (Woolen).
questions.correctAnswer: Light-colored cotton clothes in summer; dark-colored woolen clothes in winter.

proof.text: heat. You must have observed that a hot utensil kept away from the fl ame cools down after some time. What is the reason for it? The hot utensil cools down by radiating heat to its surroundings. Why is it more comfortable to wear white or light-coloured clothes during summers and dark-coloured clothes during winters? Light-coloured clothes refl ect most of the heat that falls on them, and therefore, we feel more comfortable wearing them during summers. Dark surfaces, on the other hand, absorb more heat, and therefore, we feel more comfortable with dark-coloured clothes during winters. questions.question: Draw a neat labelled diagram of Human digestive system.
questions.studentAnswer: Digestive system [Diagram with labels: buccal cavity, salivary glands, oesophagus, stomach, liver, pancreas, large intestine, small intestine, rectum, anus]
questions.correctAnswer: Neat diagram with all major parts of the alimentary canal and associated glands labeled.
questions.feedback: Diagram and labels provided but scored partial marks, likely for lack of detail or neatness.

questions.question: Case study: Nitrogen absorption in plants, abundance gas, and carbohydrate components.
questions.studentAnswer: i) Plants cannot absorb nitrogen... bacteria convert... farmers adding fertilisers... ii) Nitrogen... iii) carbon, hydrogen & oxygen.
questions.correctAnswer: i) Bacteria convert gas to usable form/fertilisers. ii) Nitrogen. iii) Carbon, hydrogen, oxygen.
questions.feedback: Excellent performance on the case study.

questions.question: Case study: Tooth decay, culprits, and number of teeth.
questions.studentAnswer: i) at least twice a day... bacteria break down sugar... ii) 32 teeth... iii) Bacteria break down the sugars... release acid... damage the teeth. This is call tooth decay.
questions.correctAnswer: i) Twice a day to remove food/bacteria. ii) 32. iii) Gradual damage to teeth caused by acids produced by bacteria.
questions.feedback: Very good understanding of dental hygiene.

questions.question: What is the ultimate source of energy for all living organisms?
questions.studentAnswer: Solar energy
questions.correctAnswer: Solar energy

questions.question: Green colour of the plant is due to the presence of ______.
questions.studentAnswer: chlorophyll
questions.correctAnswer: Chlorophyll

questions.question: Which of the following Organism is not an Autotrophs?
questions.studentAnswer: pitcher plant
questions.correctAnswer: Fungus
questions.feedback: Pitcher plants are autotrophic as they perform photosynthesis. Fungi are heterotrophic (saprotrophs).

proof.text: Other Modes of Nutrition in Plants: There are some plants which do not have chlorophyll. They cannot prepare food. Like humans and animals such plants depend on the food produced by other plants. They use the heterotrophic mode of nutrition. Example: Cuscuta (Amarbel) is a parasite.

questions.question: Pseudopodia help in the ingestion of food in _____.
questions.studentAnswer: Amoeba
questions.correctAnswer: Amoeba

questions.question: The complete digestion & absorption of food occurs in _____.
questions.studentAnswer: Small intestine
questions.correctAnswer: Small intestine

questions.question: The largest gland in human body is _____.
questions.studentAnswer: Liver
questions.correctAnswer: Liver

questions.question: No medium is required for the transfer of heat by the process of _____.
questions.studentAnswer: Radiation
questions.correctAnswer: Radiation

questions.question: ______ type of clothes do we prefer to wear in summer.
questions.studentAnswer: Light-coloured
questions.correctAnswer: Light-coloured

questions.question: Assertion (A): There are four types of teeth present in mouth. Reason (R): Incisor is a type of teeth which involved in the process of grinding the food.
questions.studentAnswer: A is true but R is false
questions.correctAnswer: A is true but R is false
questions.feedback: Correct. Incisors are used for cutting and biting, not grinding.

questions.question: Assertion (A): Food can be cooked faster in copper bottom vessels. Reason(R): Copper is the best conductor of heat.
questions.studentAnswer: Both A and R are True and R is the correct explanation of A
questions.correctAnswer: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A

questions.question: Write the differences between autotrophic nutrition & heterotrophic nutrition.
questions.studentAnswer: Defined autotrophic nutrition as self-nourishment (plants) and heterotrophic nutrition as nourishment from others (humans, animals).
questions.correctAnswer: Autotrophic: Organisms make their own food. Heterotrophic: Organisms depend on other organisms for food.

questions.question: Define conductors & give example.
questions.studentAnswer: Materials which allow heat to pass through them are called Good Conductors. Examples: Aluminium, copper.
questions.correctAnswer: Materials that allow heat to pass through them easily are conductors. Examples: metals like iron, copper, aluminum.

questions.question: Define insectivorous plants. Give 2 examples?
questions.studentAnswer: Examples: pitcher plant and venus fly trap. They trap and digest insects for extra nutrition like nitrogen, and usually grow in poor soil.
questions.correctAnswer: Plants that eat insects are called insectivorous plants. Examples: Pitcher plant, Venus flytrap.

questions.question: Explain about clinical thermometer & laboratory thermometer.
questions.studentAnswer: Clinical: 35°C to 42°C, for human body temperature, has a kink. Laboratory: -10°C to 110°C, used in labs, no kink.
questions.correctAnswer: Clinical: range 35-42°C, has a kink. Lab: range -10 to 110°C, does not have a kink.

questions.question: Mention the types of teeth with their function?
questions.studentAnswer: Listed 4 types (molars, premolars, canines, incisors) with functions. Incorrectly stated that milk teeth count is 28.
questions.correctAnswer: Incisors (cutting/biting), Canines (tearing), Premolars/Molars (chewing/grinding). Milk teeth: 20, Permanent: 32.
questions.feedback: Factual error: The count of milk teeth is 20, not 28.

questions.question: Explain about feeding & Digestion in Amoeba.
questions.studentAnswer: Described it as a microscopic organism using pseudopodia for food ingestion and digestion occurring in food vacuoles.
questions.correctAnswer: Amoeba uses pseudopodia to engulf food particles, forming a food vacuole where digestive juices are secreted to break down the food.

questions.question: Draw a neat labelled diagram of human digestive system?
questions.studentAnswer: Hand-drawn diagram including buccal cavity, oesophagus, stomach, liver, pancreas, intestines, and anus.
questions.correctAnswer: A neat diagram including all major organs of the human digestive system correctly labelled.

questions.question: Case based questions on the stomach.
questions.studentAnswer: i) Stomach is the widest part. ii) Mucus, hydrochloric acid, digestive juices. iii) Mucus protects lining; HCl kills bacteria; Digestive juices break down protein.
questions.correctAnswer: i) Stomach. ii) Mucus, hydrochloric acid, and digestive juices. iii) HCl kills bacteria and makes medium acidic; digestive juices digest protein.

questions.question: Case based questions on components of food and nutrition.
questions.studentAnswer: i) Nutrition is the mode of taking food and its utilization. ii) Carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, and minerals. iii) water, carbon dioxide, minerals.
questions.correctAnswer: i) Definition of nutrition. ii) Components (nutrients): carbohydrates, proteins, fats, etc. iii) Raw materials: water, CO2, minerals.

questions.studentAnswer: movement only

questions.question: Define Temperature? Mention the device that is used to measure temperature?
questions.studentAnswer: A temperature is an hot or cold of an body object. The device that is used to measure temperature is thermometer
questions.correctAnswer: Temperature is the degree of hotness or coldness of an object. A thermometer is used to measure it.

questions.question: What is the function of Tongue?
questions.studentAnswer: The tongue helps for chewing & swallowing
questions.correctAnswer: The tongue helps in mixing saliva with food during chewing and aids in swallowing.

questions.question: Define Nutrition? Mention the modes of nutrition?
questions.studentAnswer: The process where food is taken & used for growth, energy, body building & repair for our body. There are two types of nutrition: Auto trophic nutrients, hetero trophic nutrients.
questions.correctAnswer: Nutrition is the mode of taking food by an organism and its utilization by the body. Modes: Autotrophic and Heterotrophic.

questions.question: Why do organisms need to take food?
questions.studentAnswer: Organisms need to take food due to they are depend on each other/other trophics like (plants & animals) like we humans, animals & un-green plants.
questions.correctAnswer: Organisms need food for energy to carry out life processes, growth, and repair of damaged body parts.
questions.feedback: Grammatically weak and the explanation focuses on dependency rather than biological necessity (energy/repair).

proof.text: Heterotrophs: Animals and most other organisms take in food prepared by plants. They are called heterotrophs (heteros = other).

questions.question: What kind of clothes should we wear in summer & winter.
questions.studentAnswer: Summer:- we should wears very loose clothes & cooler clothes due to in summer heat presence is more. Cotton. Winter:- we should wear very thick & woolen clothes or cotton clothes which traps the heat. Woolen.
questions.correctAnswer: Summer: Light colored cotton clothes. Winter: Dark colored woolen clothes.
questions.feedback: Confused cotton for winter (corrected to wool by teacher). Loose clothes is a good point for summer.

questions.question: Case based study: How do plants fulfil their requirements of nitrogen? (ii) Name the abundance gas present in air? (iii) Name the components of carbohydrate?
questions.studentAnswer: i. Plants cannot absorb nitrogen in this form... bacteria... convert gaseous nitrogen into a soluble form... ii. Nitrogen... iii. carbon, hydrogen & oxygen.
questions.correctAnswer: i) Through nitrogen-fixing bacteria (Rhizobium) or fertilizers. ii) Nitrogen. iii) Carbon, Hydrogen, Oxygen.
questions.feedback: Excellent response to the case study questions.

questions.question: Case based study: (i) How many times the teeth should be cleaned and why? (ii) How many teeth are present normal health person? (iii) Define tooth decay?
questions.studentAnswer: i. at least twice a day because if we do not clean our teeth & mouth after eating many harmful bacteria... release acids... damage teeth... ii. 32 teeth... iii. Bacteria break down the sugars... release acid... damage teeth. This is call tooth decay.
questions.correctAnswer: i) Twice a day to remove bacteria/acids. ii) 32. iii) Gradual damage to teeth caused by acid from sugar-breaking bacteria.
questions.feedback: Well written answers from the text.

questions.question: Assertion (A): Food can be cooked faster in copper bottom vessels. Reason(R): Copper is the best conductor of heat.
questions.studentAnswer: Both A and R are True and R is the correct explainition of A.
questions.correctAnswer: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A

questions.question: Write the differences between autotrophic nutrition & heterotrophic nutrition.
questions.studentAnswer: Autotrophic nutrition: plants prepare their own food... Heterotrophic nutrition: organisms that cannot make their own food...
questions.correctAnswer: Autotrophs synthesize food from simple substances; Heterotrophs depend on plants/animals for food.

questions.question: Define conductors & give example.
questions.studentAnswer: Material which allows the heat to pass through them are called Good Conductors. Ex: Aluminum, copper.
questions.correctAnswer: Materials that allow heat to pass through them easily; e.g., Metals like copper and iron.

questions.question: Define insectivorous plants. Give 2 examples?
questions.studentAnswer: These plant trap and digest Insect... Ex: pitcher plant and venus fly trap.
questions.correctAnswer: Plants that trap and digest insects to fulfill their nitrogen requirements. E.g., Pitcher plant, Venus flytrap.

questions.question: Explain about clinical thermometer & laboratory thermometer.
questions.studentAnswer: Clinical thermometer used for human body (35C to 42C), has kink... Laboratory thermometer used in labs (-10C to 110C), no kink...
questions.correctAnswer: Clinical: 35°C-42°C, has kink, for body temp. Laboratory: -10°C to 110°C, no kink, for general use.

questions.question: Mention the types of teeth with their function?
questions.studentAnswer: Molars/Premolars for chewing/grinding, Canines for piercing/tearing, Incisors for cutting/biting.
questions.correctAnswer: Incisors (biting), Canines (tearing), Premolars/Molars (grinding/chewing).

questions.question: Explain about feeding & Digestion in Amoeba.
questions.studentAnswer: Amoeba uses pseudopodia for food... digestion occurs in food vacuoles.
questions.correctAnswer: Amoeba uses pseudopodia to engulf food; digestion happens in food vacuoles using enzymes.

questions.question: Draw a neat labelled diagram of human digestive system?
questions.studentAnswer: [Diagram provided with labels like Buccal cavity, Oesophagus, Stomach, Liver, etc.]
questions.correctAnswer: Labelled diagram including stomach, small intestine, large intestine, liver, pancreas.

questions.question: Case based: (i) Widest part of alimentary canal (ii) Substances released from stomach (iii) Function of acid.
questions.studentAnswer: (i) Stomach (ii) mucus, hydrochloric acid, digestive acid (iii) Acid kills bacteria, makes medium acidic.
questions.correctAnswer: (i) Stomach (ii) Mucus, HCl, digestive juices (iii) Kills bacteria and activates enzymes.
questions.feedback: Correct answers based on the passage.

questions.question: Case based: (i) Define Nutrition (ii) Components of food (iii) Raw materials for photosynthesis.
questions.studentAnswer: (i) Mode of taking food... (ii) Carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, minerals (iii) Water, CO2, minerals.
questions.correctAnswer: (i) Mode of taking and utilizing food (ii) Nutrients (Carbs, Fats, etc.) (iii) Water, CO2, minerals.
